title: Rename one symbol among a name collision (don't touch the namesake)
prompt: This repo has TWO unrelated functions both named `load`: one in src/cache.js and a different one in src/db.js. Rename ONLY the `load` defined in src/cache.js (and its callers) to `loadFromCache`. Do NOT rename or modify the `load` in src/db.js or its callers. Running `node src/index.js` must still print exactly `cache:k|db:i`. Make the edits now.
rubric: cache.js load + its caller renamed to loadFromCache
db.js load (the namesake) left completely untouched
hypothesis: Kit ON resolves `load` to a specific graph symbol and renames only its references; OFF tends to text-replace and corrupt the unrelated namesake (or miss the caller).
